packageRules.description: Vendored-from tracking. Renovate opens a normal PR bumping the digest (visible), but the vendored-resync CI check fails if upstream actually changed a vendored file and it wasn't re-synced in the PR — so a digest-only bump can't be merged blindly. Trivial bumps (upstream file unchanged) pass and are safe to merge.
prBodyNotes: ⚠️ This PR only advances the `# vendored-from:` digest. If the **vendored-resync** check fails, upstream changed a vendored file — check out this branch, re-copy the upstream file and re-apply our local adaptations (see the file's header), commit, then merge. If the check passes, upstream did not touch our vendored files and this is safe to merge as-is.

customManagers.description: ESPHome external_components git refs annotated with a renovate comment

ESPHome github:// package includes pinned with @version and a renovate comment. Version tags only; commit-SHA pins are handled by the git-refs manager below (the currentValue pattern requires a non-hex char so a 40-char SHA does not match here).

Track a GitHub repo's main-branch commit digest via git-refs. Used for esphome external_components pinned to a commit SHA (repos with no releases/tags) and for vendored-from source tracking. A move on main produces a digest-bump PR (or a dependency-dashboard entry) signalling a re-check / re-sync.
